commit.message:
Fix/r7 security hardening (#7)

* fix: main-process key management, CSP hardening, LRU eviction bug

SECURITY (3 R7 findings fixed):
- Remove decrypt oracle: deleted safe-storage-decrypt/encrypt IPC handlers.
  API keys are now stored and managed exclusively in the main process.
  Renderer never receives plaintext keys.
- Main-process key injection: ai-api-request and ai-tts-request handlers
  automatically inject auth headers (Authorization, x-api-key, xi-api-key,
  x-goog-api-key) based on request URL host. Services no longer send keys.
- CSP hardening: removed 'unsafe-inline' from script-src in production mode.
  Kept in dev mode for Vite HMR. style-src retains unsafe-inline (React needs it).
- LRU eviction: fixed history deletion bug in RedisDeviceAuthService.
  Evicted devices now preserve rate-limit history to prevent rate-limit bypass.

Key management architecture:
- Keys encrypted with safeStorage, stored in userData/api-keys.enc
- save-api-keys IPC: renderer sends keys once, main encrypts and stores
- get-api-key-status IPC: returns boolean map (which keys are configured)
- Settings shows 'Key configured' placeholder, never raw values
- getApiKeys() returns 'configured' placeholder for truthiness checks

Files: 17 changed (+308/-126), 509 tests passing (408 frontend + 101 backend)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve 4 MOE R8 findings — key deletion, state sanitization, history sweep

- Allow individual API key deletion: Settings tracks dirty fields, main
  process accepts empty strings to clear individual keys (gemini-r8-1/2)
- Sanitize React state: plaintext keys replaced with 'configured' placeholder
  immediately after save — never persist in component state (gemini-r8-3)
- Bounded fallback history: sweep stale _fallbackRequestHistory entries for
  devices no longer active (>24h expired) to prevent memory growth (codex-r8-1)
- Converted Gemini repro test into positive assertion of fix behavior

512 tests passing (411 frontend + 101 backend)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add comprehensive SECURITY.md documenting all defense layers

Catalogs every security measure across the full stack:
- API key management (main-process encryption, key injection)
- Content Security Policy (dev vs production, no unsafe-eval)
- Electron process isolation (context isolation, IPC surface)
- URL/host validation (AI, OAuth, redirect allowlists)
- OAuth security (device tokens, state params, custom protocol)
- Rate limiting (Redis Lua scripts, fallback, history preservation)
- Network security (HTTPS enforcement, CORS proxy, TTS size limits)
- Input validation (backend + frontend)
- Azure Key Vault + Managed Identity
- CI/CD (OIDC federation, approval gates, code signing)
- MOE review history (8 rounds, 3 models per round)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve 3 MOE R9 findings — race condition, atomic write, stale keyStatus

- Sweep race condition: use ICollection.Remove with reference equality to
  prevent removing a history list that was concurrently refreshed by a new
  request (gemini-r9-1)
- Atomic file write: persistApiKeys writes to .tmp then renames, preventing
  corruption on crash/power loss (gemini-r9-2)
- Settings keyStatus: derive from current config prop instead of stale
  useState, fixing race when Settings opens before async key load (opus-r9-1)

511 tests passing (410 frontend + 101 backend)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: R10 sweep race condition, dev-mode key loss, file permissions

- Move ICollection.Remove inside lock to prevent concurrent request
  from losing timestamps during stale history sweep (opus-r10-1)
- Dev mode saveApiKeys: read-merge-write instead of overwrite to
  prevent data loss when saving partial key updates (gemini-r10-1)
- Combine keysToSave and keysToDelete into single saveApiKeys call
  to eliminate race between parallel fire-and-forget calls (gemini-r10-2)
- Await saveApiKeys with error handling: show toast on failure,
  don't update state to 'configured' until save succeeds (gemini-r10-3)
- Set mode 0o600 on temp file during atomic key write (sonnet-r10-3)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add 1-minute cooldown to stale history sweep

Prevents O(N) iteration on every request when history entries exist
but haven't expired yet. Without cooldown, high-load fallback mode
causes CPU exhaustion from repeated no-op sweeps (gemini-r11-1).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Brooke Philpott <bphilpott@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

network: Pyramid tier-based connection topology

Restructures Phase 1 startup connections to follow the collateral
tier hierarchy, creating a naturally balanced and fracture-resistant
network:

          ┌──── Gold ────┐     full mesh backbone (few nodes)
         Silver ── Silver       ALL Gold + N lateral Silver peers
        Bronze  ── Bronze       N Silver (upward) + M Bronze peers
       Free  Free  Free  Free   N Bronze/Silver upward connections only

Rules per tier:
  Gold   → ALL other Gold (full mesh) + 3 Silver for downward reach
  Silver → ALL Gold + 4 lateral Silver peers
  Bronze → 5 Silver (upward) + 3 lateral Bronze peers; falls back to Gold
  Free   → 5 Bronze (upward) + 1 Silver fallback; last resort Gold

Anti-fracture guarantee: every tier always has a path upward; Gold is
fully meshed so any two nodes can always reach each other.

Also adds tier field to PeerExchangeEntry so GetPeers responses
include tier info — recipients can intelligently filter by tier
to find the right layer for their pyramid position.  GetPeers
responses are now sorted Gold-first, then by connection_count
within each tier.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Scout app build, SDK constraint, and add 2026 DrawingData (Array) to CSV (#77)

Scout couldn't build due to undeclared `http` dependency, the scan app's
SDK pin blocked all non-exact SDK versions, and the `List<int>
drawingData` field ("Array", new for 2026) was missing from every CSV
definition outside the Flutter app itself.

## Changes

**`scouting/android/pubspec.yaml`**
- Added `http: ^1.1.0` as a direct dependency — was imported in 6
`.dart` files but only present as a transitive dep, breaking clean
installs

**`scouting/scan/pubspec.yaml`**
- Relaxed SDK constraint from exact `3.8.1` → range `'>=3.4.3 <5.0.0'`
so the app can be developed and built with any compatible Dart SDK

**`scouting/data-chunk/qrcode_scanner.py`**
- Added `endgame_DrawingData` column to `CSV_HEADER` — the Flutter app
already encodes `drawingData` via `DrawingBitmaskCodec` in
`EndPoints.toCsv()`, but the Python scanner's header was out of sync,
breaking field-count validation and CSV writes

**`scouting/generate_test_data.py`**
- Added `endgame_DrawingData` column to `columns` list and row

commit.message:
discovery: fix parsing for go, npm, openssl, and aws-style CLIs

- go toolchain: recognize 'The commands are:' section header and match
  single-tab-indented subcommand lines (goTabSubcmdRe)
- npm: recognize 'All commands:' section header and parse comma-separated
  command lists, validating each token before accepting as subcommand
- openssl: recognize 'Standard commands', 'Message Digest commands', and
  'Cipher commands' section headers; parse multi-column whitespace-separated
  grids before the section-reset check fires (allGridEntries helper)
- aws: skip error-like --help output (isErrorOutput helper) so the 'help'
  positional fallback is used; strip man-page backspace bold sequences
  (stripManpageFormatting / manpageBoldRe) so '+\bo' bullet format is
  correctly parsed by awsBulletRe
- detectSection: handle parenthetical suffix headers like 'Cipher commands
  (see the enc command)' by stripping from ' (' onward before lookup
- Tests: add 3 new parse tests for go/npm/openssl formats

commit.message:
Harden fly.toml: private-only Portainer, minimal config, no public IP (#2)

* Initial plan

* Improve fly.toml based on official fly.io docs

- Fix auto_stop_machines from boolean false to documented string "off"
- Fix kill_timeout from string "5s" to integer format, increase to 10s
- Remove unnecessary processes = ["app"] (no explicit process groups)
- Add [[vm]] section for predictable Machine sizing
- Add [[restart]] policy = "always" for reliability
- Add [checks.portainer] TCP health check on port 9000
- Update README with configuration documentation

Co-authored-by: PonomareVlad <2877584+PonomareVlad@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove health check (Portainer binds to localhost only)

Fly.io health checks require the port to be available on 0.0.0.0,
but Portainer intentionally binds to 127.0.0.1:9000 for security
(only accessible via Tailscale serve).

Co-authored-by: PonomareVlad <2877584+PonomareVlad@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify fly.toml: remove services/env/concurrency, use defaults

- Remove [[services]] section entirely to prevent public IP allocation
- Remove [env] PORT (unused, hardcoded in start.sh)
- Remove retries limit for infinite restarts
- Remove memory override to use default 256 MB (shared-cpu-1x)

Co-authored-by: PonomareVlad <2877584+PonomareVlad@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Portainer health check via /api/status on port 9000

- Change Portainer bind from 127.0.0.1:9000 to 0.0.0.0:9000 in start.sh
  (safe: no [[services]] = no public IP, only reachable via 6PN/Tailscale)
- Keep HTTPS on 127.0.0.1:9443 (only accessed via tailscale serve)
- Add [checks.portainer] HTTP health check on /api/status
- Update README with health check documentation

Co-authored-by: PonomareVlad <2877584+PonomareVlad@users.noreply.github.com>

* Security fix: revert Portainer to 127.0.0.1, remove health checks

- Revert Portainer bind from 0.0.0.0:9000 back to 127.0.0.1:9000
- Remove [checks] section (requires 0.0.0.0 which would expose Portainer)
- Port 41641 remains available for Tailscale inside the VM (DERP relay mode)
- Update README to document private-only architecture

Co-authored-by: PonomareVlad <2877584+PonomareVlad@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restore [[services]] UDP 41641 for Tailscale direct connections

UDP services on Fly.io do NOT auto-allocate a public IP (only HTTP/HTTPS do).
Keeping the section preserves direct WireGuard connectivity potential
while preventing any public Portainer exposure.

Removed from original: [env] PORT, processes, [services.concurrency]
Preserved: protocol=udp, internal_port=41641, auto_stop/start=false

Co-authored-by: PonomareVlad <2877584+PonomareVlad@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove [[services]] section (no-op without IP) and delete README.md

Co-authored-by: PonomareVlad <2877584+PonomareVlad@users.noreply.github.com>

Use _byte_to_char for separators instead of store8

The my_sep/my_row_sep/my_null_marker functions used make_buffer+store8
to create 1-byte separator strings. On ARM64 with Novus V0.1.3,
back-to-back make_buffer+store8 calls can corrupt each other's buffers
due to register/memory issues.

Using _byte_to_char returns string literals from the data section,
avoiding any runtime memory allocation or store8 calls.

Requires novus-std v1.1.8+ which added bytes 1-8 to _byte_to_char.

commit.message:
network: Load-aware PeerExchange for connection distribution

Foundation for balanced mesh topology:

- Add PeerExchangeEntry { address, connection_count, is_masternode }
  and PeerExchange(Vec<PeerExchangeEntry>) message type
- Track peer connection load in PeerConnectionRegistry (peer_load DashMap)
  with update_peer_load() / get_peer_load() / get_peers_by_load() helpers
- GetPeers now responds with load-sorted PeerExchange instead of a plain
  IP list so peers can steer new connections toward underloaded nodes
- Handle incoming PeerExchange: store load data and add peer candidates
- Randomize Phase 1 masternode selection (shuffle before take(N)) so
  every node picks a different subset at startup instead of always
  hammering the same first N masternodes
- Sort Phase 3 regular peer candidates by ascending known load
- Redirect overloaded inbound connections: after handshake, if inbound
  count exceeds 70 % of MAX_INBOUND and we have 3+ alternatives, send
  PeerExchange redirect and close (never below MIN_CONNECTIONS=8)

commit.message:
Integrate cargo-delta for selective CI validation (#131)

## Summary

Integrates [cargo-delta](https://github.com/tekian/cargo-delta) to
detect which packages are impacted by changes in a PR, so CI only
validates affected packages instead of the entire workspace.

## Changes

- **delta.toml** - configuration with trip wire patterns for
workspace-wide files (Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, rust-toolchain.toml,
clippy.toml, workflow files, justfiles, etc.) and `mermaid` as a custom
include macro.
- **validation.yml** - adds a `delta` job that runs first on PRs,
analyzes baseline vs current branch, and outputs affected packages. All
downstream jobs depend on it and scope work via `package=` variable.
Push to main acts as a full workspace backstop.
- **justfile** - `target_package` now supports space-separated package
names for multi-package scoping.
- **justfiles/just_delta.just** - new recipes: `delta`,
`delta-validate`, `delta-validate-extra` for local delta workflows.
- **justfiles/just_testing.just** - `test-docs` and `run-examples`
updated to handle multi-package lists.
- **justfiles/just_quality.just** - `hack` recipe now uses
`target_package` for scoping; `coverage-package` supports multi-package.
- **justfiles/just_setup.just** - added `cargo-delta` to install-tools.
- **DEVELOPMENT.md** - documents the delta workflow for local and CI
use.

## How it works

1. **PR builds:** Delta job compares PR branch against `origin/main`,
identifies affected packages (including transitive dependents), and
downstream jobs only validate those packages.
2. **Push to main:** Backstop build validates the full workspace (no
delta).
3. **Trip wires:** Changes to workspace-wide config files trigger full
validation.
4. **Local use:** `just delta`, `just delta-validate`, `just
delta-validate-extra` for developers.

fix: double server-start and _ProgressTqdm missing get_lock()

Double server start:
- get_or_start_server() now does an HTTP health check against the
  default URL BEFORE checking the PID file.  The launcher process
  exits after daemonising so its PID was always 'dead', causing every
  second get_client() call to try starting a new server.

_ProgressTqdm tqdm error:
- _ProgressTqdm now subclasses tqdm.tqdm instead of being a bare
  shim, so huggingface_hub's thread pool can call get_lock() on it.
- Overrides only update() to forward file-count progress to the job
  callback; all other tqdm machinery is inherited.
